Barred_Coral_Snake.warning: Warning: Highly venomous. Avoid contact and seek medical help immediately if bitten.
Barred_Coral_Snake.description: The Barred Coral Snake is a small but extremely venomous species known for its bright warning coloration. Despite its size, it carries potent neurotoxic venom that can affect the nervous system. It is rarely encountered due to its secretive and burrowing lifestyle.
traits: Slender body with red, black, and yellow bands
Small head with smooth glossy scales
Barred_Coral_Snake.habitat: Found in forest floors, leaf litter, and moist soil. Often hidden under logs, rocks, or underground.
Barred_Coral_Snake.behavior: Very shy and secretive. Rarely aggressive but will bite if handled. Mostly nocturnal.
Barred_Coral_Snake.ecological_role: Controls populations of small reptiles and invertebrates, helping maintain ecological balance.
safety: Wear protective footwear in forest areas
Seek immediate medical attention if bitten

Dog_toothed_Cat_Snake.description: A slender arboreal snake with rear fangs. It is mildly venomous and primarily feeds on birds, lizards, and small mammals. It has large eyes adapted for night hunting.
traits: Slender body with brown or patterned coloration
Dog_toothed_Cat_Snake.habitat: Forests, trees, and vegetation. Often found in branches and dense foliage.
Dog_toothed_Cat_Snake.behavior: Nocturnal and agile. Moves quickly through trees while hunting prey.
Dog_toothed_Cat_Snake.ecological_role: Helps control bird and small reptile populations.

Gervais_Worm_Snake.description: A tiny burrowing snake that resembles a worm. It is completely harmless and rarely seen due to its underground lifestyle.
Gervais_Worm_Snake.habitat: Soil, leaf litter, and under rocks or logs in forest areas.
Gervais_Worm_Snake.behavior: Highly secretive and rarely seen. Feeds on small insects and larvae.
Gervais_Worm_Snake.ecological_role: Helps control insect populations and contributes to soil health.

King_Cobra.warning: Warning: Extremely dangerous. Keep a safe distance.
King_Cobra.description: The King Cobra is the longest venomous snake in the world and is known for feeding on other snakes. It is highly intelligent and capable of delivering large amounts of neurotoxic venom.
King_Cobra.habitat: Dense forests, bamboo thickets, and near water sources.
King_Cobra.behavior: Generally shy but highly defensive. Raises body and hood when threatened.
King_Cobra.ecological_role: Controls populations of other snakes, including venomous species.
safety: Seek immediate medical help if bitten

Luzon_Bronzeback_Treesnake.description: A fast-moving arboreal snake commonly found in trees. It is non-venomous and not dangerous to humans.
Luzon_Bronzeback_Treesnake.behavior: Active during the day and highly alert.

Mangrove_Cat_Snake.description: A striking black and yellow snake known for its bold coloration and arboreal lifestyle.

Mullers_Wolf_Snake.description: A small nocturnal snake often mistaken for venomous species due to its banded pattern.

North_Philippine_Temple_Pitviper.description: A tree-dwelling pit viper with heat-sensing pits used to detect prey.
North_Philippine_Temple_Pitviper.behavior: Ambush predator that remains still for long periods.

Paradise_Tree_Snake.description: A unique gliding snake capable of moving between trees by flattening its body.

Philippine_Blunt_headed_Catsnake.description: A specialized snail-eating snake with a blunt head adapted for its diet.

Philippine_Bronzeback_Treesnake.description: A slender and fast tree-dwelling snake commonly seen in forested areas.

Philippine_Cat_Snake.description: A nocturnal arboreal snake with large eyes and slender body.

Philippine_Cobra.warning: Warning: Highly dangerous. Seek immediate medical attention if bitten.
Philippine_Cobra.description: One of the most venomous snakes in the Philippines. Known for its ability to spit venom and its aggressive defensive behavior.

Philippine_Pitviper.description: A green arboreal pit viper known for its camouflage and ambush hunting style.

Red_tailed_Green_Ratsnake.description: A large green snake with a distinctive red tail. Non-venomous but may bite defensively.

Reinhardts_Lined_Snake.description: A small burrowing snake with a lined pattern, rarely seen above ground.

Reticulated_Python.description: One of the longest snakes in the world, known for its powerful constriction and striking pattern.
